> _Verbatim runtime bring-up research from the deob-runtime-bringup-design workflow (2026-06-01)._

## FINDINGS

### Run JDK (hard constraint — resolved)
- The default `java`/`javac` on this box is **JDK 26** (`/usr/bin/java`), which **removed `java.applet`** entirely — `javac` errors with "package java.applet does not exist". `GameShell extends java.applet.Applet`, so JDK 26 cannot compile or run the deob.
- **Must use JDK 17**: `/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk/{bin/javac,bin/java}`. It has `java.applet.Applet` (deprecated-for-removal warning only). 5 deob files import `java.applet`: `util/{Globals,JSBridge,Utility}.java`, `net/ClientStream.java`, `ui/FontBuilder.java` (plus `GameShell` itself).
- JDK 21 is JRE-only here (no `javac`). So the run/compile JDK is pinned to **17**.

### Display: headless box, but Xvfb is available and works
- `DISPLAY` is **unset**; `GraphicsEnvironment.isHeadless()` returns **true** by default. Under this state `new Frame()` / `Frame.getGraphics()` throw `java.awt.HeadlessException` — so the windowed `GameShell`→`GameFrame`→`getGraphics()` loop **cannot run as-is**.
- **`/usr/bin/Xvfb` and `/usr/bin/xvfb-run` are installed and verified working.** Launching `Xvfb :99 -screen 0 1024x768x24` then running with `DISPLAY=:99` flips `isHeadless()→false` and makes `Frame.getGraphics()`, `Frame.createImage()`, and `BufferedImage.getGraphics()` all succeed under JDK 17. **The full windowed run loop is viable on this box via Xvfb.**
- Windowed-run command form: `Xvfb :99 -screen 0 1024x768x24 & DISPLAY=:99 /usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk/bin/java -cp <out> client.Mudclient <nodeid> live`.
- A pure-headless harness (no Xvfb) is *only* viable for the render subsystem in isolation: `BufferedImage.getGraphics()` works headless, and `SurfaceSprite` renders into its own `int[]` framebuffer (matching the obf `dumprender/PatchAndRender.java` reflection approach — construct `Surface`/`Scene`/`World`, drive `setCamera`/`render`, read the `int[] rb`). The applet/`GameShell` run loop calls `getGraphics()` on a Frame and therefore needs Xvfb.

### The central gating fact: subsystems do NOT compile standalone — they are co-dependent with Mudclient
The task premise ("deob SUBSYSTEMS already compile, 0 errors") is **not true as committed on this branch**. Measured now under JDK 17:
- Compiling all 67 subsystem `.java` (everything except `Mudclient.java`) yields **11 errors, every one a back-reference into `Mudclient`**: `Mudclient.equipSlotJk` (`net/SocketFactory.java:666,691`), `Mudclient.ze` (`util/Timer.java:89,92,95`), `Mudclient.Jk` (`world/World.java:160`, `scene/SurfaceSprite.java:45`), and `Mudclient.a(...)` (`scene/SpriteScaler.java:332`).
- Adding a *minimal* `Mudclient` stub (static `equipSlotJk`/`ze`/`Jk`/`a`) drops it to **5 errors** — but those reveal the engine also calls **instance methods back into Mudclient**: `SurfaceSprite` calls `this.client.drawTeleportBubble/drawItem/drawNpc/drawPlayer` (renderer calls back into the game class during sprite blits), and `Mudclient.a()` actually takes a `Buffer`, not `byte[]`.
- Conclusion: the subsystems are **genuinely co-dependent with the real reconciled `Mudclient`** and cannot produce runnable bytecode without it. This matches `docs/build/findings/01` (~1,535 obf-name call sites in Mudclient) and `MILESTONE_1_RESULTS.md` (0/8 packages, the split is "architectural visibility + ~7 drift sites"). **Every runtime milestone is therefore gated on the concurrent Mudclient reconciliation completing AND compiling against these subsystems.** (Leaf files like `data/CachePath.java` do compile clean in isolation.)

### Entry point EXISTS; what must be ADDED
- **`main()` already exists**: `Mudclient.java:544` `public static final void main(String[] args)`. Args: `args[0]`=nodeid (int), `args[1]`=mode (`"live"`/`"rc"`/`"wip"`), `args[2..]`=`"members"`/`"veterans"`. It builds the client and calls `client.a(false, "local.runescape.com", ..., nodeId+7000, ...)` = the obf name for `GameShell.startApplication(...)`, which creates the `GameFrame`, wires `LoaderThread`, and starts the game thread. **No new launcher needed** — but note the port is `nodeId+7000` and host is hardcoded `"local.runescape.com"`.
- **Must ADD an RSA-modulus override.** Login (`Mudclient.java:1888`) uses static `BitBuffer.RSA_MODULUS` = the **Jagex 1024-bit key** (`ca950472…`, `BitBuffer.java:50`) + `FontBuilder.RSA_EXPONENT`=65537. The cradle's OpenRSC target uses a **different, server-generated key** (see below). Need a one-line setter to overwrite `BitBuffer.RSA_MODULUS` before login (mirrors rscplus `JConfig.SERVER_RSA_MODULUS` override).
- **Must ADD a local-cache / host config.** In standalone mode `GameShell.getCodeBase()` returns `null` (because `InputState.gameFrame != null`), and `DataStore.baseUrl` stays null → `DataStore.openStream` reads cache files **off local disk** (good for offline) but `CacheUpdater.downloadAndVerifyCrcs` expects an HTTP code base for `contentcrcs`. The standalone `startApplication` only triggers the HTTP update when `doUpdate > 20` (main passes `(byte)112` → update IS attempted against `http://127.0.0.1:port`). Need to either point at a local content host or pre-stage the cache and suppress the update. Also `CachePath.initialize` must be called (writes to `~/.jagex_cache_<slot>/` — writable here).
- **Must resolve `serverHost` for standalone.** `loadGameConfig` (`Mudclient.java:1194-1208`) sets `serverHost` from `getCodeBase().getHost()` (null in standalone) on most branches; only the `BZip.instance == fontMetrics` branch hardcodes `"local.runescape.com"` (`:1202`). Standalone path needs the host wired explicitly or it NPEs at connect (`:1839 new ClientStream(createSocket(...,serverHost),...)`).

### Server, cache, credentials — availability in THIS environment
- **Cache/content: AVAILABLE.** `rscplus/assets/content/` has the full content pack (`content0..content11` + `contentcrcs`) and `rscplus/assets/jf/` has the bitmap fonts (`h11p.jf`…`h24b.jf`) that `GameShell.loadJagex()` requires. These are the candidate local cache. (The boot also needs `jagex.jag`→`logo.tga`; presence not confirmed in assets — flag to verify.)
- **Server: NOT currently running.** Nothing listens on 43594–43596. OpenRSC source is present (`openrsc/server`, `build.gradle`, libs in `lib/`) but **not built** (no server jar). The cradle defaults to `-server localhost:43596`; OpenRSC `default.conf:21` `server_port: 43594` (43596 = the "openrsc" world variant). A server must be built+started before M3.
- **RSA: BLOCKER for login.** OpenRSC `net/rsc/Crypto.java` **generates a fresh random RSA keypair on first boot** (writes `client.pem`/`server.pem` to its CWD) — **none are committed anywhere** in the repo. The cradle's `v235.DefaultServerRSA()` (`proto/v235/rsa.go:25`) hardcodes a specific captured 512-bit modulus (`76342505…`) and the session handshake (`session/handshake.go:39`) uses it without fetching live. So **all three** (cradle, deob, OpenRSC) must agree on one modulus: whatever the live OpenRSC instance generated. The deob's baked Jagex `ca950472…` key will **never** authenticate against OpenRSC — its `BitBuffer.RSA_MODULUS` MUST be overridden to the live server's modulus (and the modulus is only known after the server boots and writes its keys).

### Milestone ladder and gating
- **M0 (prerequisite, currently UNMET): Mudclient reconciled + the 68-file tree compiles to bytecode under JDK 17.** Gates everything. The 11 measured errors are all Mudclient back-refs; needs the concurrent workflow to finish.
- **M1 (boots + inits window): GATED on M0 + Xvfb.** `Xvfb :99` + `DISPLAY=:99` + JDK 17 + `java client.Mudclient <nodeid> live`. `startApplication` builds the `GameFrame`; should init without crashing. Add the local-cache/host config so `loadJagex` finds fonts/logo.
- **M2 (cache + login/title screen): GATED on M1 + staged cache.** Point `DataStore.baseUrl`/cache at `rscplus/assets/content` (verify `jagex.jag` present), suppress or satisfy the `contentcrcs` HTTP update. Reaches the login UI (`drawLoginScreen`).
- **M3 (connect + log in): GATED on M2 + running OpenRSC + RSA override.** Build+start OpenRSC (`build.gradle`), read the modulus it writes to `client.pem`, set `BitBuffer.RSA_MODULUS` to it, set `serverHost`=localhost and port to match (43594/43596), create an account on that server. Login wire format is proven byte-faithful (M1: 48/48 constants, RSA/ISAAC/XTEA byte-identical), so the protocol floor is solid.
- **M4 (live world frame): GATED on M3.** Cache (content/maps/models) must be complete and matched to the server revision so `loadRegion`/`drawWorld` succeed. Renderer is unproven for pixel-equivalence (M1: 0 measured, 4 known drift sites at `scene/GameModel.java:302/304/346`).
- **M5 (playable): GATED on M4** + input loop + full opcode-handler correctness (also unmeasured).

### Primary blockers, honestly flagged
1. **M0: the tree does not compile** — subsystems are co-dependent with the un-landed reconciled Mudclient (11 measured errors, all Mudclient back-refs; renderer calls instance methods on the game class). Nothing runs until this closes.
2. **RSA key mismatch** — deob bakes the Jagex key; OpenRSC self-generates a random key with no committed pem; the cradle's captured modulus only matches one specific instance. Requires booting OpenRSC, capturing its live modulus, and overriding `BitBuffer.RSA_MODULUS` (a setter must be added). Login is impossible otherwise.
3. **No server running / not built** — OpenRSC source present but unbuilt; nothing on 4359x.
4. **JDK pin** — must force JDK 17 for every `javac`/`java`; the default JDK 26 cannot even compile `java.applet`.

Relevant files: entry `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/reference/rsc-client/src/client/Mudclient.java:544` (main), `:1194-1208` (host/port), `:1888` (RSA login); shell `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/reference/rsc-client/src/client/shell/GameShell.java:576` (startApplication), `:272` (run loop), `:619` (loadJagex), `:810` (getGraphics); RSA `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/reference/rsc-client/src/client/net/BitBuffer.java:50`; cache `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/reference/rsc-client/src/client/data/{CachePath.java,DataStore.java:178}`; server RSA `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/openrsc/server/src/com/openrsc/server/net/rsc/Crypto.java:94-124`; cradle config `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/cmd/cradle/main.go:77`; captured key `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/proto/v235/rsa.go:25`; handshake `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/session/handshake.go:39`; candidate cache `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/rscplus/assets/content/` + `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/rscplus/assets/jf/`; authoritative compile-state record `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/reference/rsc-client/docs/build/MILESTONE_1_RESULTS.md`.
